channel.title: robot.tv News
channel.description: Video-first robot news and robotics news from robot.tv covering humanoids, physical AI, quadrupeds, and deployment signals.

item.title: Inside Project Kobe: Amazon's plan to build Walmart-style supercenters powered by warehouse robots and AI
item.description: Amazon is developing large-format retail supercenters, internally codenamed Project Kobe, that integrate warehouse robotics and AI. This move signals a direct operational shift to compete with established big-box physical retail models.

item.title: US lawmakers to introduce bill to ban government use of Chinese robots
item.description: U.S. lawmakers are preparing legislation to prohibit federal agencies from using Chinese-made robots. The move directly targets government procurement and supply chains.

item.title: 3 robotics trends from NVIDIA GTC 2026
item.description: NVIDIA's 2026 GTC conference highlighted three key robotics trends. These developments signal where computational demands and investment are shifting for next-generation systems.

item.title: Lucid Bots raises $20M to keep up with demand for its window-washing drones
item.description: Lucid Bots has secured $20 million in new funding. The capital injection is aimed at scaling production to meet rising demand for its commercial window-cleaning drones.

item.title: Meet the machines: The latest generation of robots
item.description: On the latest generation of robots entering the market. The coverage highlights new hardware and software capabilities from emerging companies.

item.title: No arms, no legs, no problem: the robots taking over retail logistics
item.description: A new wave of wheeled, armless robots is automating material transport in warehouses and retail backrooms. These systems prioritize simple navigation and high-volume movement over complex manipulation.

item.title: Amazon acquires humanoid developer Fauna Robotics
item.description: Amazon has acquired Fauna Robotics, a developer specializing in humanoid systems. The move signals a strategic push into advanced automation for logistics and fulfillment.

item.title: Amazon just bought a startup making kid-size humanoid robots
item.description: Amazon has acquired a startup developing child-sized humanoid robots. The move signals a strategic push into a new form factor for potential logistics or consumer applications.

item.title: OpenAI is scrapping the Sora app to chase bigger AI goals
item.description: OpenAI has discontinued its Sora application, shifting focus to broader AI development. This move signals a strategic pivot away from a specific product to concentrate on core research and infrastructure.

item.title: TI partners with NVIDIA to accelerate robot deployments
item.description: Texas Instruments and NVIDIA have announced a partnership to accelerate robot deployment. The collaboration aims to streamline development and integration processes for robotics systems.

item.title: Agile Robots becomes the latest robotics company to partner with Google DeepMind
item.description: Agile Robots has entered a partnership with Google DeepMind. This collaboration signals a strategic move to integrate advanced AI research with commercial robotics hardware.

item.title: China's open-source dominance threatens US AI lead, US advisory body warns
item.description: A U.S. advisory body warns that China's lead in open-source AI development poses a direct challenge to American technological leadership. This shift could reshape the foundational tools available for robotics and automation globally.

item.title: Amazon acquires robotic doorstep delivery provider RIVR
item.description: Amazon has acquired RIVR, a startup specializing in robotic doorstep delivery. The move signals a direct push to automate the final, most complex segment of the logistics chain.

item.title: Building tomorrow: How Bedrock Robotics is changing the construction industry
item.description: Bedrock Robotics is a useful startup-execution story because it ties robotics ambition to a concrete industry workflow, where customer adoption depends on operating fit rather than broad platform hype.

item.title: Amazon acquires Rivr, maker of a stair-climbing delivery robot
item.description: Amazon has acquired Rivr, a startup developing a stair-climbing delivery robot. The move signals a deeper investment in last-mile automation for complex urban environments.

item.title: Inside China’s robotics revolution
item.description: China's robotics rise looks bigger than a single humanoid headline. Manufacturing depth, policy support, and industrial ambition may be combining into a broader competitive advantage.

item.title: How humanoid robots joined this factory's workforce
item.description: A factory-floor humanoid story matters most when it shows how robots are being placed into mixed human-robot workflows with a clearer operating role, not just as future-of-work spectacle.

item.title: Inside the new ‘Living Lab’ advancing agricultural robotics
item.description: A new 'Living Lab' for agricultural robotics is being framed as a real-world testing environment for field robots in active farm operations, where developers can work through the messy conditions that lab demos miss.

item.title: Gecko Robotics lands the largest U.S. Navy robotics deal yet
item.description: Gecko Robotics has secured the largest robotics contract to date from the U.S. Navy. This deal marks a significant milestone for the company's expansion into defense infrastructure.

item.title: BEDEX 2026: Sweden receives Blaze interceptor UAVs from Origin Robotics
item.description: Sweden has taken delivery of Blaze interceptor UAVs from Origin Robotics. The handover occurred at the BEDEX 2026 defense exhibition.

item.title: Memories AI is building the visual memory layer for wearables and robotics
item.description: Memories AI is developing a visual memory layer for wearables and robotics. The startup aims to enable devices to understand and recall their visual experiences.

item.title: Travis Kalanick launches a new company called Atoms focused on robotics
item.description: Travis Kalanick, the former Uber CEO, has launched a new robotics company named Atoms. The venture's specific focus and initial product details remain undisclosed.

item.title: Don’t miss Neuralink pioneer Noland Arbaugh keynote at the 2026 Robotics Summit
item.description: Noland Arbaugh, a pioneering Neuralink patient, is confirmed as a keynote speaker for the 2026 Robotics Summit. His firsthand experience with a brain-computer interface offers a unique perspective for the robotics community.

item.title: Robot Dogs Are Protecting Data Centers. Operators Are Seeing Payoffs.
item.description: Legged robots are now patrolling data center floors, moving beyond pilot programs into active security and inspection roles. Early operators report measurable returns on investment from these deployments.

item.title: Humanoid robotics developers must address a wide range of applications
item.description: Argues humanoid robotics developers must look beyond a single killer app. Success hinges on designing versatile platforms capable of addressing a wide range of industrial and commercial applications.

item.title: Rivian’s RJ Scaringe thinks we’re doing robots all wrong
item.description: Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe critiques the prevailing approach to robotics development. He argues for a fundamental shift in how the industry builds and deploys autonomous systems.

item.title: Why Travis Kalanick believes humans are on the verge of a 'golden age'
item.description: Travis Kalanick argues that a convergence of technologies is setting the stage for a 'golden age' for humanity. His perspective, reported by Business Insider, frames this as a pivotal moment for innovation and commercial application.

item.title: Data security is the foundation of trust in physical AI
item.description: Emphasizes data security as the critical foundation for trust in physical AI systems. This focus addresses core operational and commercial vulnerabilities as robots move into sensitive environments.

item.title: 'I never left': Travis Kalanick launches new robotics company Atoms with manifesto
item.description: Travis Kalanick has launched a new robotics company called Atoms, accompanied by a public manifesto. The move marks his return to the tech sector with a focus on robotics commercialization.

item.title: Uber co-founder Kalanick launches Atoms in specialized robotics push
item.description: Uber co-founder Travis Kalanick has launched Atoms, a new venture focused on specialized robotics. The move signals a significant strategic push into a targeted segment of the automation market.

item.title: Three companies demonstrate global commercialization potential at AW 2026
item.description: Three robotics companies demonstrated significant progress toward global commercialization at AW 2026. Their showcases highlight a tangible shift from pilot projects to scalable, real-world deployment.

item.title: STMicroelectronics plans robots, retraining to avoid closures
item.description: STMicroelectronics is deploying robotics and retraining programs to keep its European chip plants open. The move counters high energy costs and aims to preserve its manufacturing footprint.

item.title: Elon Musk says Tesla expects to increase its human workforce as AI and robotics boost productivity
item.description: Tesla plans to expand its human workforce, citing productivity gains from AI and robotics. This hiring strategy contrasts with typical automation narratives.

item.title: Rhoda AI exits stealth with $450M to train robots from video
item.description: Rhoda AI has emerged from stealth with a $450 million funding round. The capital is earmarked for training robots using video data.

item.title: MassRobotics, NVIDIA, and AWS announce second Physical AI Fellowship cohort
item.description: MassRobotics, NVIDIA, and AWS have selected the second cohort for their Physical AI Fellowship. The program supports early-stage startups working on embodied AI and robotics.

item.title: Humanoid robotics maker Sunday reaches $1.15B valuation to build household robots
item.description: Humanoid robotics startup Sunday has reached a $1.15 billion valuation. The capital will fund its push to develop robots for household applications.

item.title: Rivian spin-out Mind Robotics raises $500M for industrial AI-powered robots
item.description: Mind Robotics, a spin-out from Rivian, has secured $500 million in funding. The capital is designated for developing industrial robots powered by AI.

item.title: Teradyne sues Chinese cobot maker over UR software
item.description: Teradyne has filed a lawsuit against a Chinese collaborative robot manufacturer, alleging infringement of Universal Robots' software. The legal action centers on the core programming that defines the UR cobot platform's functionality.

item.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
item.description: Qualcomm has entered a strategic partnership with Neura Robotics, focusing on integrating its AI compute platforms into Neura's cognitive robotics systems. This collaboration aims to accelerate the development of more capable and efficient robots.

item.title: OpenAI robotics head resigns over Pentagon deal
item.description: A senior robotics departure at OpenAI matters because leadership changes around defense-linked decisions can reshape how teams, partners, and observers read the company's priorities in physical AI.
